examples.title: Union of Languages
examples.description: A pseudocode program that implements the union of two languages.
examples.code:
FUNCTION union(L1, L2)
  RETURN L1 + L2
END FUNCTION
examples.explanation: This program takes two languages L1 and L2 as input and returns their union.

examples.title: Intersection of Languages
examples.description: A pseudocode program that implements the intersection of two languages.
examples.code:
FUNCTION intersection(L1, L2)
  RETURN L1 ∩ L2
END FUNCTION
examples.explanation: This program takes two languages L1 and L2 as input and returns their intersection.

examples.title: Concatenation of Languages
examples.description: A pseudocode program that implements the concatenation of two languages.
examples.code:
FUNCTION concatenation(L1, L2)
  RETURN L1 × L2
END FUNCTION
examples.explanation: This program takes two languages L1 and L2 as input and returns their concatenation.

examples.title: Kleene Star of a Language
examples.description: A pseudocode program that implements the Kleene star of a language.
examples.code:
FUNCTION kleeneStar(L)
  RETURN L*
END FUNCTION
examples.explanation: This program takes a language L as input and returns its Kleene star.
